headline: 'Football brings us together!': Night of euphoria and tension in an electrified Paris after PSG wins Champions League
description: As many as 20,000 people gathered on the Champs-Elysées on Saturday to celebrate Paris Saint-Germain's victory over Arsenal (1-1, 4-3 on penalties). In the greater Paris area, 283 people were arrested by 1:30 am on Sunday following clashes with police.

headline: Gaza, Iran, Mali: The dangers of the world's rapidly shrinking media access
Macron hosts his final Choose France event against a backdrop of stalled reindustrialization
'Trump and Netanyahu have turned their own blindness into a weapon of mass destruction'
description: The mere consideration of Mahmoud Ahmadinejad returning to lead Iran speaks volumes about the intellectual collapse of the Israeli-American alliance, observes historian Jean-Pierre Filiu in his column.

headline: Colombia goes out to vote in high-stakes presidential election
description: The frontrunners in the race are debating the best course of action to end a recent surge in violence by armed groups. Polling stations are open from 8 am to 4 pm local time, and results are expected a few hours after polls close.

headline: Simone Bellotti brings a poetics of restraint to Jil Sanders
description: Noticed for his work at Bally, where he helped reinvigorate the brand, the Italian designer has found at Jil Sander, where he has served as creative director for the past year, a setting to express his taste for minimalism and restrained melancholy.

headline: Antonin Baudry, the filmmaker liberating Charles de Gaulle
description: Baudry's new diptych 'De Gaulle' follows the leader of Free France during World War II. In his film, the former French diplomat aims to shake off the general's image as a rigid savior.

headline: Putin's war fuels the growing role of Russia's FSB intelligence agency
Israel plants flag on medieval castle, escalates Lebanon ground operation

San Antonio Spurs overpower Oklahoma City Thunder to advance to NBA Finals
Palestinian refugees forced to flee the southern Lebanon: 'All my life, I have only known displacement'
The oil crisis suits TotalEnergies' CEO perfectly

Cheesecake, panna cotta, chocolate fondant: When olive oil finds its way into dessert
French sociologist, philosopher and intellectual provocateur Edgar Morin has died aged 104
description: Committed to connecting different fields of knowledge to develop his 'complex thought,' this former member of the Resistance and deeply humanist intellectual advocated for a revolution of consciousness. He died in Paris on Friday, his wife confirmed to Le Monde.

headline: Who will manage to capture the 'middle' electorate Macron seized 10 years ago?
description: Among French President Emmanuel Macron's political heirs, and even on the left, some are starting to believe that the presidential campaign could play out in the middle, amid the collapse of France's former governing parties.

headline: Why German trains are never on time anymore
description: The German rail network has deteriorated over the past 20 years, to the point that Deutsche Bahn has become a source of national embarrassment.

headline: Ukraine's lesson in resilience
By backtracking on environmental progress, Macron is 'betraying' the French, former members of the Climate Convention write
Political scientist Robert Kagan: 'War in Iran risks permanently weakening the position of the US in the world'
